page: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. System memory totals 384.0 GB, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk configurations.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in several compute and memory metrics. The server achieves top-tier status in PassMark CPU tests, including extended instructions and encryption, alongside a strong multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also rank in the top tier. Memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 345.73 GB/sec for large-block read operations. For database workloads, the instance demonstrates top-tier Redis SET operations at 152.07 million operations per second and strong PassMark database operations. Static web serving throughput is also top-tier at 124.31 GB/sec. Large language model inference benchmarks show strong prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models, though text generation speeds remain in the average category.

From a resource density perspective, the D96s_v7 offers high compute density with 96 dedicated vCPUs, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for highly parallelized workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff is the complete reliance on remote storage, as there is no local SSD capacity. This server is optimized for enterprise-grade general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded data processing, high-throughput web serving, memory-cached databases like Redis, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine in the Dsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores, 384.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-threaded decompression, PassMark CPU encryption, and Redis SET operations, which reach 152.07 million operations per second. Memory bandwidth is strong, with large-block reads measured at 345.73 GB/sec. While prompt processing for large language models is strong, text generation speeds are average. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-density, CPU-bound tasks. It is best suited for high-throughput web serving, parallel data processing, and large-scale database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance without local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-density, 96-vCPU general-purpose instance featuring advanced Intel Xeon processing and top-tier multi-threaded performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB RAM powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor
Top-tier performance in multi-threaded decompression, cryptography, and Redis SET operations
Strong memory bandwidth with large-block read speeds up to 345.73 GB/sec
Premium Storage capable configuration with no local SSD storage or bundled GPUs
Suited for high-throughput web serving, parallel data processing, and enterprise databases
